---
title: "Probability"
author: "Var Kurapati"
format: html
execute:
  echo: false
---

```{r}
#| message: false
library(tidyverse)
```

```{r}
#| cache: true
throw_dice <- function(n, weights = rep(1/6, 6)) {
  die1 <- sample(1:6, size = n, replace = TRUE, prob = weights)
  die2 <- sample(1:6, size = n, replace = TRUE, prob = weights)
  return(die1 + die2)
}
```


```{r}
#| cache: true
log_lr <- function(rolls) {
  loaded_probs <- c(0.14, 0.14, 0.14, 0.14, 0.14, 0.30)
  log(loaded_probs[rolls] / (1 / 6))
}
```

```{r}
# Set seed for reproducibility
set.seed(10)

# Run the simulation 1,000 times
stopping_times <- map_int(1:1000, function(i) {
  # Draw a long sequence of 200 rolls from the loaded die
  rolls <- sample(1:6, size = 200, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.14, 0.14, 0.14, 0.14, 0.14, 0.30))
  
  # Calculate roll-by-roll posterior probabilities
  log_odds <- cumsum(log_lr(rolls))
  posterior <- 1 / (1 + exp(-log_odds))
  
  # Find the first roll index where posterior crosses 0.95
  which(posterior >= 0.95)[1]
})

# Plot a histogram of the 1,000 stopping times
tibble(stopping_time = stopping_times) |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = stopping_time)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 2, fill = "grey50", color = "white") +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Stopping Times to Reach 95% Confidence",
    subtitle = "Based on 1,000 simulated sequences of loaded die rolls",
    x = "Number of Rolls Required",
    y = "Frequency"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```
